examples: Please carry on with your work.
She carried on talking despite the noise.
We'll carry on after lunch.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To reach the same point, level, or standard as someone else.
examples: I need to catch up with my classmates.
He ran faster to catch up with us.
She's trying to catch up with all her reading.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To become happier, or to make someone happier.
examples: Cheer up! Things will get better.
The sunshine cheered everyone up.
I sent her flowers to cheer her up.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To tidy a place and make it clean and neat.
examples: Please clear up the kitchen after dinner.
We cleared up the living room before guests arrived.
I'll clear up while you rest.
I came across an old photo yesterday.
She came across a useful article online.
We came across a quiet café in town.
When did you come back from Italy?
He came back from work late.
Call me when you come back from holiday.

phrasalVerbs.definition: Used to tell someone to hurry up.
examples: Come on, we're going to be late!
Come on, the bus is leaving.
Come on! Move faster.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To be published or become available for people to buy or see.
examples: Her new book comes out next month.
That film came out in 2019.
When does the next issue come out?

phrasalVerbs.definition: To draw a line through written words to show they are wrong or not needed.
examples: Cross out the wrong answer.
He crossed out the sentence and rewrote it.
Please cross out your old address.

fillInExercises.sentence: Even after the interruption, she decided to ___ ___.
I missed two classes, so I need to ___ up with the group.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Catch up with' means to reach the same level as others.

fillInExercises.sentence: I found this recipe by chance—I ___ ___ it online.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Come across' means to find by chance.

fillInExercises.sentence: Please ___ up your bedroom before dinner.
___ ___! The train leaves in three minutes.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Come on' is used to tell someone to hurry.

fillInExercises.sentence: Their new single ___ out next Friday.
If this answer is wrong, just ___ it ___.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Cross out' means draw a line through text.

fillInExercises.sentence: You look sad. I hope this joke ___ you up.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Cheer up' means become happier or make happier.

lines.speaker: Nora
lines.text: Welcome back! When did you [come back from|come back (from)] Greece?

explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: *come back (from)* — To return from a place.
explanationContent.sentenceBreakdown: Welcome back! When did you come back from Greece?
explanationContent.commonMistake: Do not confuse this with other items from the lesson — here you need *come back (from)* (*come back (from)*).
